topic.what: Polymorphic backdoor attacks against semantic communication systems using intensity-based trigger poisoning
topic.how: Multi-effect poisoning-training framework with graded-intensity triggers that enable dynamic output control based on trigger intensity, plus semantic smoothing defense with certified robustness
topic.why_matters: First polymorphic SC backdoor addresses monomorphic limitations; enables diverse malicious outputs from single backdoor; defense provides first certified robustness guarantee for SC backdoors

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of attacks achieving targeted malicious outputs
characteristics.evidence: Tables across MNIST/F-MNIST/CIFAR-10/ImageNet with ASR>88% even at CR=1/12
characteristics.context: Multiple targets T; varies with compression rate and poisoning rate

characteristics.what_itasures: Degradation in transmission quality for benign samples due to attack
characteristics.evidence: Tab.VIII shows max 2.39 dB across all datasets and compression rates
characteristics.context: Minimal impact on benign transmissions; varies by dataset complexity

characteristics.what_it_measures: Attack success rate after applying semantic smoothing defense
characteristics.evidence: Tabs.IX-X show defense reduces ASR to near 0% across all systems
characteristics.context: Effective against all tested SC architectures under 25dB and 5dB SNR

characteristics.what_it_measures: PSNR degradation from applying semantic smoothing to benign data
characteristics.evidence: Tabs.IX-X show minimal ∆PSNR across all configurations
characteristics.context: Negligible impact on transmission quality even in low-SNR conditions

characteristics.evidence: Tab.XI shows mean 1.52ms (std 0.42ms) across all SC systems
characteristics.context: Well below real-time wireless system thresholds (5-10ms)

characteristics.what_itasures: Classification accuracy degradation after applying defense
characteristics.evidence: Tab.XII shows avg reduction 0.17 percentage points
